subject: [PATCH v3 02/10] nfsd: fix UAF in async copy cancel and shutdown

2026-07-10.patch_summary: This patch fixes a use-after-free (UAF) bug in the NFS daemon's async copy cancel and shutdown functionality. The issue arose from concurrent access to the async copy object, leading to double-putting of nfsd_file objects and freeing them prematurely. To fix this, the patch modifies the teardown lifetime by unlinking the async copy under a lock, dropping the list-membership reference after stopping the kthread, and pinning the task_struct to ensure safe kthread_stop(). Additionally, it removes redundant list_del fixups and ensures that s2s_cp_stateids entries are properly cleaned up. The patch also takes a copy reference in nfsd4_send_cb_offload() to prevent concurrent teardown from freeing the copy while the callback is still in flight.
